search_queries_used: official kidney care guideline chronic kidney disease follow-up
site:dha.gov.ae chronic kidney disease follow-up guideline
site:nice.org.uk chronic kidney disease follow-up guidance
candidate_sources_rejected: adjacent kidney care guidance generalized into a complete chronic kidney disease follow-up pathway
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Only the reviewed exact domains apply; remaining chronic kidney disease follow-up content lacks exact support.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with relevant chronic kidney disease follow-up history, findings and clinician decisions entered.
setting_applicability: Partial for adult CKD evaluation and longitudinal care; treatment and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
UAE_applicability: KDIGO guidance requires UAE laboratory, prescribing, nephrology and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The cited official documents and exact registered sections were reviewed on 2026-07-18.
superseded_check: The registered official-source status was checked; no newer replacement was identified in the source record.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports CKD detection and chronicity review for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports evaluation of cause and kidney-function context for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports GFR and albuminuria monitoring for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports medicine stewardship for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports qualified specialist kidney-care referral context for chronic kidney disease follow-up; no decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

unresolved_source_gaps: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Cause, stage, GFR and ACR trends, blood pressure, complications, symptoms, medicines, adherence, examinations, tests, diagnosis, treatment and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chronic kidney disease follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
